frameworks.name: distributed systems view
frameworks.one_line: WM storage spans multiple interacting brain regions rather than one

frameworks.name: sensory recruitment hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: visual cortex contributes to but does not solely house visual WM

frameworks.name: non-sensory (frontoparietal) WM account
frameworks.one_line: claim that visual cortex is non-essential for visual WM storage

core_question: Is visual working memory storage localized to a single region, or distributed across multiple brain regions?
key_assumptions: WM storage and its neural substrates can be dissociated from broader WM processes
evidence from multiple regions can be integrated into a single account
visual regions can carry storage-related signals, not only sensory ones
key_findings: inconsistencies identified in claims that visual cortex is non-essential for visual WM
wide array of brain regions show involvement in visual WM
interpretations: visual regions play a critical role in visual WM storage
WM storage is distributed, so focus should shift to how regional representations complement and interact
